attachment_text: Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Limited has informed the Exchange that the Board of Directors of the Company at its meeting held on January 28, 2013, had approved the following: (1) To focus on the high growth Indian Ports & Logistics Sector and maintain its leadership position in India, the Board of Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Limited ("APSEZ") has in-principle decided to divest its significant stake in entities controlling the Abbot Point Coal Terminal in Queensland, Australia to the Adani family, subject to requisite approvals, formalities and clearances, at a valuation determined by an independent valuer. This divestment will further enhance the financial strength of APSEZ in order to pursue its plans to acquire / set up new ports & logistics assets in India.

attachment_text: Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Limited has informed the Exchange that the Board of Directors of the Company at its meeting held on May 15, 2013, inter alia, has approved the following :(1) The Company had initiated and recorded the divestment of its entire holding equity holding in of Adani Abbot Point Terminal Holdings Pty Limited (AAPTHPL) and entire Redeemable Preference Shares holding in of Mundra Port Pty Ltd (MPPL) representing Australia Abbot Point operations to promoter Company, Abbot Point Port Holdings Pte Ltd, Singapore for consideration of AUD 235.71 million. The Company entered Share Purchase Agreement ('SPA') on March 30, 2013 to sell its holdings in AAPTHPL and MPPL. In terms of the SPA the conditionality as regards regulatory and lenders approvals was obtained subsequent to balance sheet date except in respect of approval from one of the lenders who have given specific line of credit.The Company, based on the legal counsel opinion, concluded that on the date of signing of SPA, AAPTHPL and MPPL cease to be subsidiaries of the Company w.e.f. March 31, 2013 and accordingly not been consolidated as per provisions of Accounting Standard 21 "Consolidated Financial Statements" notified in Companies (Accounting Standards) Rules, 2006.
company_name: Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Limited

attachment_text: Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Limited has informed the Exchange that the Company has submitted the Red Herring Prospectus with Registrar of Companies, Gujarat on May 31, 2013 for the proposed issue of upto 66,657,520 equity shares of Rs. 2 each being offered by way of Institutional Placement Programme and the Registrar of Companies, Gujarat has issued the Memorandum acknowledging the receipt of the same on May 31, 2013.  Further, the Finance Committee of Board of Directors of the Company has by a resolution dated June 01, 2013, fixed the price band at Rs. 148 to Rs. 158 per equity share for the proposed issue of up to 66,657,520 equity shares of Rs. 2/- each of the Company under the provision of Chapter VIII - A of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2009, as amended, to qualified institutional buyers (the "Issue").   The Issue will open and close on June 04, 2013.

attachment_text: The Exchange had sought clarification from Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Limited with respect to a news item captioned "Adani Ports bags Ennore container terminal project." The Company has clarified that "information/ declaration on the same were not official and it was not appropriate on the part of the company to communicate to Stock Exchange without any official intimation. We have now received the official intimation from Ennore Port Trust Authority and were supposed to intimate the same to Stock Exchanges in compliance clause 36 of the Listing Agreement. Accordingly, we hereby inform that the Company has received Letter of Award from Ennore Port Limited for Development of Container Terminal at Ennore Port on DSFOT basis. Further, we would like to inform that the company has not made any press report on the captioned matter."
company_name: Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone Limited
